name: Iowa SNAP (Food Assistance Program)
description: Iowa's food assistance program providing monthly EBT benefits to purchase groceries. Administered by the Iowa Department of Health and Human Services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal SNAP income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 160% FPL for most households in Iowa

name: Iowa Medicaid (Iowa Health Link)
description: Iowa's Medicaid program providing health coverage for eligible low-income residents through managed care organizations. Covers doctor visits, hospital care, prescriptions, dental, and mental health.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income adults, children, pregnant women, elderly, and individuals with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Adults up to 138% FPL under Medicaid expansion; children up to 302% FPL

name: Iowa Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary wage replacement benefits to workers who lost their jobs through no fault of their own. Administered by Iowa Workforce Development.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost employment through no fault of their own and meet wage/work requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Must have earned sufficient wages during the base period

name: Iowa Finance Authority (IFA)
description: Provides affordable housing resources including down payment assistance, first-time homebuyer programs, rental assistance, and housing counseling for Iowa residents.
eligibilitySummary: Low to moderate income individuals and families seeking affordable housing
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program and county; income limits based on AMI

name: Iowa Legal Aid
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income Iowans statewide in matters including housing, family law, public benefits, consumer issues, and employment.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals at or below 125% of the federal poverty level
incomeLimitNotes: Generally 125% FPL or below; some programs may have different limits

name: IowaWORKS (Workforce Development Centers)
description: Iowa's workforce development system offering job search assistance, training programs, resume help, and career counseling through centers statewide.
eligibilitySummary: All Iowa residents seeking employment or career development services

name: Iowa LIHEAP (Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program)
description: Provides financial assistance to help low-income households pay heating bills. Administered through the Iowa Department of Health and Human Services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households with home energy costs, prioritizing elderly, disabled, and young children
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 200% FPL

name: Iowa 211
description: Free, confidential referral service connecting Iowans with local health and human services resources including food, housing, utilities, and crisis support.
eligibilitySummary: All Iowa residents seeking assistance or referrals
languages: English, Spanish, and other languages via interpreter

name: Food Bank of Iowa
description: Feeding America affiliate serving 55 counties in central and eastern Iowa. Distributes food through a network of 600+ partner agencies and programs.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in central and eastern Iowa

name: Northeast Iowa Food Bank
description: Feeding America affiliate serving 16 counties in northeast Iowa including the Cedar Rapids and Waterloo areas. Provides food distribution through partner agencies.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in northeast Iowa

name: Food Bank for the Heartland (Western Iowa)
description: Feeding America affiliate serving 21 counties in western Iowa and Nebraska. Provides food distribution through partner agencies and mobile pantry programs.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in western Iowa

name: Iowa Department of Veterans Affairs
description: Assists Iowa veterans and their families in obtaining federal and state benefits including healthcare, disability compensation, education, and the Iowa Veterans Home.
eligibilitySummary: Veterans, active duty service members, and their dependents residing in Iowa

name: Iowa Crisis Line (Your Life Iowa)
description: Statewide crisis line providing immediate support for mental health emergencies, substance use crises, problem gambling, and suicidal thoughts.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Iowa experiencing a mental health or substance use crisis

name: Iowa Child Care Assistance
description: Provides financial assistance to help low-income families pay for childcare so parents can work, attend school, or participate in training programs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income working families with children who need childcare
incomeLimitNotes: Family income at or below 145% FPL initially; up to 225% FPL to maintain

name: Iowa FIP (Family Investment Program / TANF)
description: Iowa's TANF cash assistance program providing temporary financial help and supportive services to low-income families with children to achieve self-sufficiency.
eligibilitySummary: Very low-income families with dependent children who participate in employment activities
incomeLimitNotes: Very low income thresholds; varies by family size

name: Iowa WIC Program
description: Women, Infants, and Children supplemental nutrition program providing healthy foods, nutrition education, and breastfeeding support for eligible participants.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants and children up to age 5

name: Des Moines Area Regional Transit Authority (DART)
description: Public transit system serving the Des Moines metropolitan area with fixed-route bus service, express routes, paratransit, and on-demand options.
eligibilitySummary: Available to all riders in the Des Moines metro area; reduced fares for seniors and disabled

name: Cedar Rapids Transit
description: Public transit system serving the Cedar Rapids metropolitan area with fixed-route bus service and paratransit options.
eligibilitySummary: Available to all riders in the Cedar Rapids area

name: Iowa Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides energy efficiency improvements to low-income homes to reduce energy costs. Includes insulation, air sealing, and heating/cooling system upgrades.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters, with priority for elderly, disabled, and families with children
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 200% FPL

name: Iowa Vocational Rehabilitation Services
description: Provides vocational rehabilitation services for Iowans with disabilities including job training, placement assistance, assistive technology, and career counseling.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals with physical or mental disabilities that create barriers to employment

name: Des Moines Municipal Housing Agency
description: Provides affordable public housing and Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) for low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals in the Des Moines area.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals in the Des Moines area
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by family size; generally at or below 80% AMI for admission

name: Iowa Domestic Violence Hotline
description: Statewide hotline providing crisis intervention, safety planning, shelter referrals, and support for victims of domestic violence through the Iowa Coalition Against Domestic Violence.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing or affected by domestic violence in Iowa
languages: English, Spanish, and other languages via interpreter

name: Iowa College Aid
description: Administers state financial aid programs including Iowa Tuition Grant and Iowa Vocational-Technical Grant to help Iowa students afford post-secondary education.
eligibilitySummary: Iowa residents pursuing post-secondary education at eligible institutions
incomeLimitNotes: Iowa Tuition Grant is need-based; determined by FAFSA

name: Iowa Adult Education and Literacy
description: Provides free GED preparation, adult basic education, and English as a Second Language programs through community colleges and other providers across Iowa.
eligibilitySummary: Adults who need basic education, GED preparation, or English language instruction

name: Community Action Agencies of Iowa
description: Network of community action agencies providing anti-poverty services including emergency assistance, housing, energy, transportation, and workforce development.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families throughout Iowa

name: Iowa Head Start
description: Federally funded early childhood education programs promoting school readiness for children from birth to age 5 in low-income Iowa families.
eligibilitySummary: Families with children birth to age 5 at or below federal poverty guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: 100% FPL or below; foster and homeless children automatically eligible

name: Iowa Lifeline Telephone Assistance
description: Provides discounts on monthly telephone or broadband service for qualifying low-income Iowa households to maintain access to essential communications.
eligibilitySummary: Households participating in SNAP, Medicaid, SSI, or with income at or below 135% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: 135% FPL or participation in qualifying federal assistance program

name: DMARC (Des Moines Area Religious Council) Food Pantry Network
description: Operates 14 food pantries across the Des Moines metro area providing emergency food assistance to families and individuals facing food insecurity.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in the Des Moines metropolitan area

name: Iowa Hawk-I (Healthy and Well Kids in Iowa / CHIP)
description: Iowa's Children's Health Insurance Program providing affordable health coverage for uninsured children in families earning too much for Medicaid.
eligibilitySummary: Uninsured children under 19 in families with income above Medicaid limits but below CHIP thresholds
incomeLimitNotes: Children in families up to 302% FPL

name: United Way of Central Iowa
description: Provides community programs and funding for education, financial stability, and health services. Operates OpportUNITY program for Des Moines metro residents.
eligibilitySummary: Residents of the central Iowa region

name: Iowa Rent and Utility Assistance
description: State emergency rental and utility assistance program helping Iowa tenants maintain housing stability during financial hardship and prevent eviction.
eligibilitySummary: Renters who have experienced financial hardship and are at risk of eviction
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 80% AMI
